_notes: Per-worker tool subsets (Step 2.3). Each router work-category (config/router.json)
maps to a MINIMAL allowlist of Pi built-in tool names, tuned to the work so a
worker starts with only the tools it needs (least privilege) instead of all 7.
Pi's built-in tool set is exactly 7 names (confirmed from the pi-coding-agent
source: createAllTools) — there is NO 'glob' tool; file-finding is 'find':
read  - read a file
write - create/overwrite a file
edit  - in-place edit of an existing file
bash  - run a shell command
grep  - search file CONTENTS (ripgrep)
find  - find FILES by name/glob (the glob-equivalent)
ls    - list a directory
Pi's own groupings for reference: read-only = [read,grep,find,ls];
coding = [read,bash,edit,write]; all = the 7 above.
piToolArgs() turns a category's 'tools' array into Pi CLI flags:
non-empty -> ['--tools','read,grep,find']  (allowlist)
empty []  -> ['--no-tools']                (disable everything)
Unknown / missing category falls back to 'default' (a safe read-only set).
JSON (not YAML) is used deliberately: the repo is zero-dep and already stores
config as JSON (config/router.json), so no YAML parser is needed. The plan named
tool-profiles.yaml; JSON is the zero-dep-consistent deviation.

orchestrate.rationale: Coordinator: reads specs/state, maintains state & task/lock files (write), navigates the repo (grep/find). No code edits, no shell — it delegates work, it does not run it.

plan.rationale: Planner: reads code and specs to produce a plan. The plan is its RETURNED output (a message), not a file it mutates — so read-only navigation is enough. No write/edit/bash.

code.rationale: Implementer: the fullest set. Reads, edits and writes source, runs builds/tests (bash), and searches the tree (grep/find). ls omitted — find/grep cover navigation.

analyze.rationale: Analyst: reads code/data and may run read-only commands to compute or inspect (bash), but its deliverable is findings, not file changes — so NO edit/write.

draft.rationale: Content drafter: reads references and writes the draft (write). No bash/edit — it authors new prose/content rather than surgically patching existing files or running commands.

extract.rationale: Extraction: pulls structured facts FROM sources it locates (find) and reads (read/grep). Output is returned as data, not written to disk — so read-only, NO bash/edit/write.

classify.rationale: Classifier: reads the item and (optionally) greps for signals, then returns a label. The most minimal set — no file discovery, mutation, or shell needed.

summarize.rationale: Summarizer: reads the content (and greps within it) and returns a summary. Read-only and minimal — no find/write/edit/bash.

code-review.rationale: Reviewer: reads the diff/code, searches the tree (grep/find), and may run tests/linters (bash) — but must NOT mutate the code it reviews, so NO edit/write. Its output is comments.

lateral.rationale: Lateral/alternative-idea explorer: surveys the codebase read-only (read/grep/find) to propose different approaches. No mutation or shell — it ideates, it does not execute.

default.rationale: Safe fallback for any unknown/unmapped category: Pi's read-only group. Least privilege — an unrecognised worker can inspect but never mutate the repo or run shell.
